results.expected_behavior: Suspicious or Likely scam. It must identify the untrusted payment link, urgency, payment demand, and threat.

candidate.simple_explanation: This message claims to be from Pakistan Post but uses a suspicious URL ending in '.example' and demands immediate payment to prevent destruction, which is a common phishing tactic.
red_flags: Request for payment via unverified link
safe_next_steps: Do not click the link or provide any payment information.
Contact Pakistan Post directly using their official phone number or website found on their verified social media pages.
Report the message to your mobile carrier or local authorities.

judgment.reason: The candidate correctly identifies the message as a 'Likely scam'. It accurately grounds its claims in the provided text by citing the suspicious '.example' domain, the urgency, the threat of destruction, and the payment demand. The safe next steps explicitly advise against clicking the link or paying, and suggest contacting the official entity through verified channels, avoiding any untrusted contact details. All fields are present, useful, and internally consistent.

results.expected_behavior: Suspicious or Likely scam. The screenshot shows a failed-delivery parcel message with a clickable rearrangement link. Branding alone must not be treated as proof of legitimacy.

candidate.simple_explanation: This looks like a common courier scam message. The sender name is generic ('POST OFFICE') and the message asks you to click a link to 'rearrange' delivery. Official Pakistan Post messages usually come from a specific branch or use a different format. Do not click the link.
red_flags: Urgent request to click a link
Common scam script about failed delivery
safe_next_steps: Do not click the link in the message.
Contact your local Pakistan Post branch directly using a known phone number.
Check your official Pakistan Post account or website for delivery status.
Report the message to your mobile carrier if it was sent via SMS.
candidate.reply_draft: This message appears to be a common courier scam. Do not click the link. Contact Pakistan Post directly to verify your parcel status.

judgment.reason: The candidate correctly identifies the message as a likely scam, aligning with the reference expectation. The explanation is grounded in visible evidence (generic sender name, suspicious link). The safe next steps are appropriate and avoid engaging with the suspicious content.

results.expected_behavior: Suspicious or Likely scam. It offers an iPhone/gifts and redirects the recipient to a visible WhatsApp phone number.

candidate.simple_explanation: This message claims you won electronics and gifts but asks you to contact a different number on WhatsApp. This is a common pattern used to trick people into sharing personal info or paying fees.
red_flags: Request to contact via a different phone number
No official sender name or logo visible
safe_next_steps: Do not click any links or reply to this message
Do not contact the number 03405140972
Do not share personal information, OTPs, or bank details
Report the message to your carrier (Telenor) or block the sender
If you received a similar message before, check your bank statements for unauthorized charges

judgment.reason: The candidate correctly identifies the message as a likely scam. The explanation accurately reflects the content of the image (unsolicited prize offer, redirection to WhatsApp). The safe next steps explicitly advise against contacting the suspicious number found in the image and do not recommend engaging with the sender. All fields are useful and consistent.

results.expected_behavior: Likely scam because it requests an OTP and threatens an account block.

candidate.simple_explanation: This message claims to be from HBL but asks the user to send an OTP to a 'support agent.' Legitimate banks never ask for OTPs via SMS or ask users to send them to anyone other than the official app or website.
red_flags: Instructs to contact 'support agent' outside official channels
Uses urgency to pressure the user
safe_next_steps: Do not send the OTP.
Do not reply to the message.
Verify the alert directly through the official HBL mobile app or website.
Report the message to HBL customer care.

judgment.reason: The candidate correctly identifies the message as a likely scam based on the request for an OTP and the threat of account blocking, which aligns with the reference expectation. The explanation is grounded in the provided text. The safe next steps correctly advise against sharing the OTP or engaging with the sender, and suggest verifying through official channels. All fields are present, useful, and internally consistent.

safe_next_steps: Do not click the link or provide any personal information.

Report the message to your mobile carrier or relevant authorities.
